nodes.title: Timeout rejects only if work did not finish yet
nodes.why: The settled check avoids rejecting after the promise already resolved.
nodes.alternative: Use Promise.race without explicit settled tracking.
nodes.tradeoff: A bit more code, but cleanup and winner logic stay explicit.

nodes.title: Clear timer when promise settles first
nodes.why: Clearing the timer prevents a late timeout callback from running unnecessarily.
nodes.alternative: Leave timeout running and rely only on settled flag.
nodes.tradeoff: One extra cleanup step, but less wasted work and clearer intent.

nodes.title: Fail fast if signal is already aborted
nodes.why: If cancellation already happened, we should reject immediately and skip extra setup.
nodes.alternative: Always attach listeners first and let abort event flow later.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly more branching, but faster and cleaner in aborted cases.

nodes.title: Attach abort listener once and remove on settle
nodes.why: This keeps abort wiring leak-free for both success and error paths.
nodes.alternative: Keep listener attached until garbage collection.
nodes.tradeoff: Needs explicit removeEventListener calls, but avoids stale listeners.

nodes.title: Abort combined signal once, then detach all listeners
nodes.why: One shared abort path keeps behavior consistent and cleans all source listeners.
nodes.alternative: Handle cleanup separately in each signal listener.
nodes.tradeoff: Central helper adds structure, but makes composed cancellation easier to reason about.
